protocol_definition.name: Shared Blackboard
protocol_definition.message_flow: Agents post structured contributions to a shared blackboard; Writer synthesizes.
protocol_definition.visibility: Every agent sees the task and current full blackboard.
protocol_definition.termination: Stop after all rounds or budget exhaustion, then Writer output.

### Market Overview
In the current landscape of remote work, effective communication and collaboration tools are essential for small companies. Slack and Microsoft Teams are two of the most popular platforms, each offering unique features tailored to enhance team productivity. This comparison will focus on their target users, core communication features, collaboration capabilities, strengths, and limitations.

### Product Comparison Table

| Product          | Target Users                     | Core Communication Features                     | Collaboration and Integrations                     | Main Strength                     | Main Limitation                  |
|------------------|----------------------------------|------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------|----------------------------------|
| Slack            | Small to medium-sized teams      | Channels, direct messaging, voice/video calls  | Integrates with numerous third-party apps         | User-friendly interface           | Limited video conferencing features |
| Microsoft Teams  | Businesses of all sizes          | Chat, video conferencing, file sharing         | Deep integration with Microsoft 365 applications   | Comprehensive feature set         | Can be overwhelming for new users |

### Recommendation
Based on the comparison, **Slack** is recommended for a small remote company that requires daily messaging, file sharing, video meetings, and basic integration with other workplace tools. Its user-friendly interface and focus on communication make it ideal for smaller teams looking for a straightforward solution. While Microsoft Teams offers a more comprehensive feature set, its complexity may not be necessary for smaller organizations, and its video conferencing capabilities are not as robust as dedicated platforms.

### Source List
- Slack official website: [Slack](https://slack.com)
- Microsoft Teams official website: [Microsoft Teams](https://www.microsoft.com/en-us)

### Market Overview
In the current landscape of remote work, effective communication and collaboration tools are essential for small companies. Slack and Microsoft Teams are two of the most popular platforms, each offering unique features tailored to enhance team productivity. This comparison will focus on their target users, core communication features, collaboration capabilities, strengths, and limitations.

### Product Comparison Table

| Product          | Target Users                     | Core Communication Features                     | Collaboration and Integrations                     | Main Strength                     | Main Limitation                  |
|------------------|----------------------------------|------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------|----------------------------------|
| Slack            | Small to medium-sized teams      | Channels, direct messaging, voice/video calls  | Integrates with numerous third-party apps         | User-friendly interface           | Limited video conferencing features |
| Microsoft Teams  | Businesses of all sizes          | Chat, video conferencing, file sharing         | Deep integration with Microsoft 365 applications   | Comprehensive feature set         | Can be overwhelming for new users |

### Recommendation
Based on the comparison, **Slack** is recommended for a small remote company that requires daily messaging, file sharing, video meetings, and basic integration with other workplace tools. Its user-friendly interface and focus on communication make it ideal for smaller teams looking for a straightforward solution. While Microsoft Teams offers a more comprehensive feature set, its complexity may not be necessary for smaller organizations, and its video conferencing capabilities are not as robust as dedicated platforms.

- Microsoft Teams official website: [Microsoft Teams](https://www.microsoft.com/en-us)

### Market Overview
In the current landscape of remote work, effective communication and collaboration tools are essential for small companies. Slack and Microsoft Teams are two of the most popular platforms, each offering unique features tailored to enhance team productivity. This comparison will focus on their target users, core communication features, collaboration capabilities, strengths, and limitations.

### Product Comparison Table

| Product          | Target Users                     | Core Communication Features                     | Collaboration and Integrations                     | Main Strength                     | Main Limitation                  |
|------------------|----------------------------------|------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------|----------------------------------|
| Slack            | Small to medium-sized teams      | Channels, direct messaging, voice/video calls  | Integrates with numerous third-party apps         | User-friendly interface           | Limited video conferencing features |
| Microsoft Teams  | Businesses of all sizes          | Chat, video conferencing, file sharing         | Deep integration with Microsoft 365 applications   | Comprehensive feature set         | Can be overwhelming for new users |

### Recommendation
Based on the comparison, **Slack** is recommended for a small remote company that requires daily messaging, file sharing, video meetings, and basic integration with other workplace tools. Its user-friendly interface and focus on communication make it ideal for smaller teams looking for a straightforward solution. While Microsoft Teams offers a more comprehensive feature set, its complexity may not be necessary for smaller organizations, and its video conferencing capabilities are not as robust as dedicated platforms.

### Market Overview
In the current landscape of remote work, effective communication and collaboration tools are essential for small companies. Slack and Microsoft Teams are two of the most popular platforms, each offering unique features tailored to enhance team productivity. This comparison will focus on their target users, core communication features, collaboration capabilities, strengths, and limitations.

### Product Comparison Table

| Product          | Target Users                     | Core Communication Features                     | Collaboration and Integrations                     | Main Strength                     | Main Limitation                  |
|------------------|----------------------------------|------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------|----------------------------------|
| Slack            | Small to medium-sized teams      | Channels, direct messaging, voice/video calls  | Integrates with numerous third-party apps         | User-friendly interface           | Limited video conferencing features |
| Microsoft Teams  | Businesses of all sizes          | Chat, video conferencing, file sharing         | Deep integration with Microsoft 365 applications   | Comprehensive feature set         | Can be overwhelming for new users |

### Recommendation
Based on the comparison, **Slack** is recommended for a small remote company that requires daily messaging, file sharing, video meetings, and basic integration with other workplace tools. Its user-friendly interface and focus on communication make it ideal for smaller teams looking for a straightforward solution. While Microsoft Teams offers a more comprehensive feature set, its complexity may not be necessary for smaller organizations, and its video conferencing capabilities are not as robust as dedicated platforms.
